channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: A police officer testifies that an ex-prosecutor never obstructed her in the Ahmaud Arbery case
item.description: BRUNSWICK, Ga. (AP) — The case against a former Georgia district attorney charged with discouraging police from arresting the man who killed Ahmaud Arbery in 2020 took a blow Thursday as a police supervisor testified that the accused prosecutor never obstructed her during the investigation.
item.dc:creator: RUSS BYNUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A food bank netted a huge haul of 13,000 fresh salmon. The catch? The fish were still alive
item.description: AUBURN, N.Y. (AP) — A New York food bank was offered a huge donation of fresh fish this month — but it came with a catch.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL HILL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: No survivors after collision between American Airlines jet, Army helicopter near DC
item.description: Everyone aboard an American Airlines jet carrying 60 passengers and four crew members that collided with an Army helicopter was feared dead in what was likely to be the worst U.S. aviation disaster in almost a quarter century, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Anna Chen, KRON4

item.title: Trump buyout plan faces legal, logistical roadblocks
item.description: The Trump administration’s efforts to shrink the federal government by offering what amounts to a buyout to federal employees is raising questions about its implementation and legality.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Passenger jet with 64 aboard collides with Army helicopter while landing at Reagan Airport near DC
item.description: ARLINGTON, Va. (AP) — An American Airlines jet carrying 60 passengers and four crew members collided Wednesday with an Army helicopter while landing at Ronald Reagan National Airport near Washington, prompting a large search-and-rescue operation in the nearby Potomac River.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, TARA COPP and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Elite university attended by Trump’s son cracks down on left-wing student agitators
item.description: Almost a dozen students have been suspended for a year from NYU for previous protests as President Trump's no-nonsense approach to campus protests takes hold.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Billion-dollar Mexican cartel ops disrupted by Trump's border crackdown as US issues do-not-travel warning
item.description: The U.S. State Department is warning travelers of improvised explosive devices (IEDs) found along dirt roads near an area of northeastern Mexico that borders McAllen, Texas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Video captures fiery crash between American Airlines flight and Army Blackhawk
item.description: Editor's note: The video may appear graphic to some viewers. (NEXSTAR) - Images from the EarthCam video service captured images of what appears to be the fiery crash of an American Airlines jet over the Potomac River Wednesday evening.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, Fox 5

item.title: Video captures fiery crash between American Airlines flight and Army Blackhawk
item.description: Editor's note: The video may appear graphic to some viewers. (NEXSTAR) - Images from the EarthCam video service captured images of what appears to be the fiery crash of an American Airlines jet over the Potomac River Wednesday evening.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Regional jet carrying 64, military helicopter on training flight collide near Reagan National Airport
item.description: A regional jet carrying 60 passengers and four crew members collided with a military helicopter on Wednesday night as it approached Washington Reagan National Airport. The flight was en route from Wichita, Kan., to Washington, D.C., American Airlines said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Elvia Limon, WGN 9

item.title: Are we all aliens? NASA's returned asteroid samples hold the ingredients of life from a watery world
item.description: Asteroid samples fetched by NASA hold not only the pristine building blocks for life but also the salty remains of an ancient water world..
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Reagan National Airport crash: Military Black Hawk helicopter collides midair with American Airlines jet
item.description: An Army Black Hawk helicopter collided midair with an American Airlines flight from Wichita, Kansas, at Reagan National Airport on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former US Sen. Bob Menendez gets 11 years in prison for taking bribes and acting as agent of Egypt
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Former U.S. Sen. Bob Menendez was sentenced Wednesday to 11 years in prison for accepting bribes of gold and cash and acting as an agent of Egypt — crimes his lawyer said he’s been mocked for as “Gold Bar Bob.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Flu map: These states are now at CDC's highest activity level
item.description: The map of flu activity across the United States continues to darken, both figuratively and literally, as case numbers spike upward in most states, according to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC).
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KRON4

item.title: First independently developed jet breaks the sound barrier over the California desert
item.description: A sleek white aircraft became the first independently developed jet to break the sound barrier Tuesday, tearing through the air tens of thousands of feet above the Mojave Desert and a crowd of delighted onlookers.
item.dc:creator: HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 2 dogs get new homes after surviving plane crash that killed rescue flight pilot
item.description: Two dogs that survived an animal-rescue flight crash that killed the pilot have found new homes as the late aviator's friends work to honor him by helping more pets, a shelter director said Wednesday. The Nov.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Too little, too late: Residents say they were in the dark as fire spread with no evacuation order
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — When disaster strikes, government emergency alert systems offer a simple promise: Residents will get information about nearby dangers and instructions to help them stay safe.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER L. KELLER, CLAUDIA LAUER, AMY TAXIN and REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump orders preparation of Guantánamo Bay facility to house migrants
item.description: President Trump signed a memo on Wednesday to prepare a massive facility at Guantánamo Bay to be used to house deported migrants. Trump had earlier teased he intended to do so during a signing ceremony for an immigration-related bill.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Florida Republican introduces bill to add Trump to Mount Rushmore
item.description: The Interior Department would be in charge of undertaking Trump’s addition to the South Dakota landmark if approved by Congress.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Search underway for inmate who escaped via ventilation system: North Carolina sheriff
item.description: Authorities say the man's cellmates helped him escape and then stuffed a mattress to make it look like he was still sleeping.
item.dc:creator: Mekaela Muck, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Scottie Scheffler details his hand injury from making ravioli and is ready to get back to golf
item.description: PEBBLE BEACH, Calif. (AP) — Scottie Scheffler felt frustrated sitting at home for two tournaments he normally plays while recovering from glass puncturing the palm of his right hand while making ravioli. A big part of him was thankful the injury wasn't worse.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump approval rating slips after flurry of executive actions: Survey
item.description: President Trump's approval rating slipped after his first week back in the Oval Office, where he issued a slew of executive orders.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, Fox 5

item.title: Capitol Police arrest man with Molotov cocktails attempting to target Johnson, Hegseth, Bessent
item.description: The U.S. Capitol Police arrested a man with Molotov cocktails and knives who was allegedly attempting to kill House Speaker Mike Johnson and President Trump’s two recently confirmed nominees, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth and Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: WATCH: Miles-long cluster of dolphins captured on drone video
item.description: A miles-long cluster of dolphins has been filmed leaping and gliding across Carmel Bay off California, forming an unusual “super pod” of more than 1,500 of the marine creatures.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Live updates: White House rescinds funding freeze, RFK Jr. faces tough questions
item.description: President Trump's executive actions, including a freeze on federal grants and a buyout offer to federal employees, have drawn criticism from Democrats and some Republicans, while his pick for HHS Secretary, Robert F. Kennedy, Jr.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, ABC 4

item.title: Trump makes moves to expand power, sparking chaos, possible constitutional crisis
item.description: Just a little over a week into his second term, President Donald Trump is taking steps to maximize his power, sparking chaos and what critics contend is a constitutional crisis as he challenges the separation of powers that have defined American government for more than 200 years.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Brittney Griner plans to sign with the Atlanta Dream, ending her 11-year run with Phoenix
item.description: Brittney Griner plans to sign with the Atlanta Dream, marking the end of her 11-year career with the Phoenix Mercury.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Alyssa Thomas heading to Mercury from Sun in trade, AP source says
item.description: The Phoenix Mercury were finalizing a deal to acquire Alyssa Thomas from the Connecticut Sun, a person familiar with the trade told The Associated Press on Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Lions agree to hire Broncos' John Morton as OC and promote Kelvin Sheppard to DC, AP source says
item.description: The Detroit Lions have agreed to hire Denver Broncos assistant John Morton as offensive coordinator and promote Kelvin Sheppard to defensive coordinator, according to a person familiar with the situation.
item.dc:creator: LARRY LAGE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: A$AP Rocky's accuser says he was stunned and furious when his old friend pulled a gun on him
item.description: A$AP Rocky's accuser, former friend and the key witness at his trial testified Tuesday that their relationship had been fraying for years, but he was “furious” and flabbergasted when Rocky pulled a gun on him on the streets of Hollywood.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DALTON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: US education agency investigating Denver schools over all-gender bathroom
item.description: DENVER (AP) — The U.S. Education Department said Tuesday it was investigating Denver Public Schools for alleged discrimination after the district converted a girl’s restroom on the second floor of a high school into an all-gender restroom while leaving another bathroom exclusive to boys.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Largest documented tuberculosis outbreak in US history hits Kansas, officials say
item.description: The Kansas Department of Health and Environment (KDHE) says Kansas is facing the largest documented tuberculosis outbreak in American history.
item.dc:creator: Colter Robinson, Fox 5

item.title: California water regulators deny Trump's claim that US military 'turned on the water' in the state
item.description: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — California water officials said this week there's no truth to President Donald Trump's assertion that the U.S. military has entered California and “turned on the water.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIE AUSTIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Fewer than half in new poll trust Trump, RFK Jr. to make right recommendations on health issues
item.description: Fewer than half of Americans in a new poll say they trust President Trump or his health-related nominees to make recommendations on the nation's pressing health care issues.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Ousted IG: Trump firings 'a potentially existential threat' to government oversight
item.description: One of 17 government watchdogs ousted by President Trump said that the move was “a potentially existential threat” to a crucial government oversight tool.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, WGN 9

item.title: Watch a miles-long cluster of dolphins captured on drone video
item.description: A miles-long cluster of dolphins has been filmed leaping and gliding across Carmel Bay off the central coast of California, forming an unusual “super pod” of more than 1,500 of the marine creatures.
item.dc:creator: HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: States lose access to Medicaid payment portal amid Trump federal aid freeze
item.description: State Medicaid programs across the country are reporting they've lost access to federal payment portals one day after President Trump announced a freeze on federal grants and aid.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, WFLA, NBC
